refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates.' High safety criticality demands explicit audit and change control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and operator identity; (c) all updates are logged with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline record is complete and auditable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, mission context hash).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain managed history of all baseline and calibration updates with change reason, approver, and rollback capability.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability query interface to link any recorded requirement back to originating worksite input and approval chain.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests mission context via API, executes requirement-derivation algorithms (physics-based cycle time, energy models, availability calculations), records baselines to a versioned database, and exposes traceability queries.
DP-B: Distributed Derivation – Embed requirement-derivation logic in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules separately, with eventual consistency synchronization to a shared baseline store.

DP-C: Manual Derivation with Template Library – Site Manager manually selects performance templates based on mission type; system records selections and maintains history without algorithmic derivation.
DP-D: Hybrid Derivation – Automated derivation engine for standard mission profiles; manual override and custom calibration for non-standard scenarios, with unified baseline and history management.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with versioned baseline database and audit-logged history.

potential_sources: Mission context ingestion couples to Site Manager input interface (Tasking); decoupling via well-defined API schema mitigates.
Baseline recording couples to persistent storage; decoupling via database abstraction layer mitigates.
Traceability queries couple to audit log schema; decoupling via query API mitigates.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because it centralizes derivation logic and baseline management in a single, well-bounded module. Interfaces are clearly defined (mission context in, baseline + traceability out). No circular dependencies detected. DP-B would exhibit higher coupling due to distributed logic and synchronization overhead. DP-C would couple tightly to manual operator workflow, reducing automation and auditability. DP-D adds complexity but is viable if standard profiles dominate; DP-A is simpler and more maintainable.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (e.g., JSON payload from Site Manager system).
ASSUMPTION: Performance derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy, availability models) are well-defined and validated for NGHE equipment classes.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or inconsistent, derived requirements may be invalid; mitigation: input validation and fallback to conservative defaults.
RISK: Derivation algorithms may require frequent recalibration as equipment or worksite conditions change; mitigation: versioned algorithm library and change-control process.
RISK: High safety criticality demands that all derivation steps and baseline changes are auditable; mitigation: immutable audit log and cryptographic signing of baselines.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend adding explicit requirement: 'Derivation engine shall validate mission context completeness and reject incomplete inputs with diagnostic error message.' Also recommend: 'All baseline records shall be cryptographically signed to prevent tampering and ensure audit integrity.'

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It owns the Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A), including mission context ingestion, requirement derivation algorithms, baseline database, history management, and traceability query interface. The module interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation for operational context, and with Safety Services for safety-critical requirement validation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager via Tasking interface. Format: structured mission profile with metadata.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational telemetry (actual cycle times, energy consumption, availability metrics) from Vehicle and Teleoperation to validate and recalibrate performance baselines.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends derived safety-critical requirements (safety margins, availability thresholds) to Safety Services for validation. Receives safety constraints and approval status back.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Publishes baseline records and traceability queries to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders via Network Link for audit and compliance reporting.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of system-wide performance requirement derivation and baseline management. All derivation logic, baseline database, and history management are contained within this module. Interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are well-defined and minimal. Diffusion risk is low: only mission context ingestion (Tasking) and telemetry feedback (Telemetry) cross boundaries, both via standard interfaces.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing missions and reporting telemetry, not for deriving system-level performance requirements. Assigning DP-A to Vehicle would scatter requirement derivation logic across multiple vehicle instances, creating inconsistency and maintenance burden. High diffusion risk: each vehicle would need its own derivation engine, leading to version skew and audit trail fragmentation.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and control, not for system-level performance requirement management. Assigning DP-A to Teleoperation would couple requirement derivation to operator workflow, reducing automation and auditability. High diffusion risk: tele-operator actions would directly influence baseline records, creating audit and safety compliance issues.

Safety Services is responsible for safety validation and monitoring, not for performance requirement derivation. While Safety Services must validate derived safety-critical requirements, the derivation logic itself belongs in NGHE Solution. Assigning DP-A to Safety Services would conflate requirement derivation with safety validation, reducing separation of concerns. Moderate diffusion risk: safety-specific requirements could be derived locally, but general performance requirements would still need to be derived elsewhere.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner of the Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) because: (1) it is the system-level integration point with visibility to mission context, vehicle telemetry, and safety constraints; (2) it centralizes requirement derivation logic, eliminating duplication and version skew; (3) it provides a single, auditable baseline database and history; (4) it decouples requirement derivation from vehicle operation (Vehicle) and tele-operation (Teleoperation), improving maintainability and safety compliance.
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution module may become a 'god object' if too many system-level functions are assigned to it. Mitigation: establish clear sub-module boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline Manager, Traceability Query Service) with internal interfaces.

RISK: Mission context ingestion (Tasking interface) may be incomplete or inconsistent, causing invalid requirement derivation. Mitigation: implement input validation and fallback logic in NGHE Solution.

RISK: Telemetry feedback loop may introduce circular dependencies if Vehicle or Teleoperation modules depend on derived requirements to operate. Mitigation: ensure derived requirements are published as read-only reference data, not as control inputs.

RISK: Safety Services validation of derived safety-critical requirements may introduce approval bottlenecks. Mitigation: establish clear SLAs for safety validation and implement asynchronous approval workflow.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Recommend explicit sub-module decomposition within NGHE Solution: (1) Mission Context Ingestion Module (owns Tasking interface), (2) Requirement Derivation Engine (owns algorithms), (3) Baseline Manager (owns versioned database), (4) Traceability Query Service (owns audit log and query API). This prevents NGHE Solution from becoming a monolith and enables independent testing and deployment of each sub-module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR CR-NGHE-005 clearly specifies what the system must do: derive, record, and manage performance requirements from worksite mission context with full traceability and history. Selected DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all FR elements: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) baseline recording with metadata, (3) managed history with audit trail, (4) traceability queries. No gaps or misalignments detected. DP-A is implementable and testable.

diffusion_risks: Mission context ingestion (Tasking interface) is a boundary-crossing dependency; mitigation: API contract and input validation.
Telemetry feedback loop (Telemetry interface) may introduce circular dependencies; mitigation: ensure telemetry is used only for validation/recalibration, not for control.
Safety Services validation (SafetyAlert interface) may introduce approval bottlenecks; mitigation: asynchronous workflow and clear SLAs.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner of DP-A with containment score 95/100. All derivation logic, baseline database, and history management are contained within NGHE Solution. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimal. No diffusion of core logic across multiple modules. Recommended sub-module decomposition within NGHE Solution to prevent monolithic design.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible via: (1) unit tests of derivation algorithms against known mission contexts and expected requirement outputs; (2) integration tests of mission context ingestion and baseline recording; (3) audit log inspection to confirm all updates are logged with reason, timestamp, and approver; (4) traceability query tests to confirm chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline record is complete. High safety criticality requires formal verification of derivation algorithms and cryptographic signing of baselines.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. Sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) are allocated to specific sub-modules within NGHE Solution (Mission Context Ingestion, Derivation Engine, Baseline Manager, History Manager, Traceability Query Service). Each sub-FR has a clear owner and verification method.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit requirement: 'Derivation engine shall validate mission context completeness and reject incomplete inputs with diagnostic error message.'
Add explicit requirement: 'All baseline records shall be cryptographically signed to prevent tampering and ensure audit integrity.'
Add explicit requirement: 'Traceability query interface shall support filtering by date range, operator, mission context, and change reason for audit compliance.'
Establish clear SLAs for Safety Services validation of derived safety-critical requirements to prevent approval bottlenecks.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic and requires further functional decomposition. The refined FR specifies five distinct sub-functions: (1) mission context ingestion, (2) requirement derivation, (3) baseline recording, (4) history management, (5) traceability queries. Each sub-FR is a candidate for the next decomposition level. DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) is sound and well-coupled, but its realization requires detailed design of sub-modules and internal interfaces. Proceeding to the next level will clarify ownership, interfaces, and implementation details for each sub-function.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input via Tasking interface.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, mission context hash) to versioned database.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain managed history of all baseline and calibration updates with change reason, approver, and rollback capability.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability query interface to link any recorded requirement back to originating worksite input and approval chain.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (e.g., JSON payload from Site Manager system).
Performance derivation algorithms (cycle time, energy, availability models) are well-defined and validated for NGHE equipment classes.
Safety Services is available and responsive for validation of derived safety-critical requirements.
Telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules is reliable and timely for baseline validation and recalibration.
NGHE Solution module has sufficient computational capacity to execute derivation algorithms in real-time or near-real-time.
open_risks: Mission context may be incomplete or inconsistent, causing invalid requirement derivation; mitigation: input validation and fallback to conservative defaults.
Derivation algorithms may require frequent recalibration as equipment or worksite conditions change; mitigation: versioned algorithm library and change-control process.
High safety criticality demands immutable audit trail and cryptographic signing of baselines; mitigation: implement cryptographic signing and tamper-detection.
NGHE Solution module may become a 'god object' if too many system-level functions are assigned to it; mitigation: establish clear sub-module boundaries.
Safety Services validation may introduce approval bottlenecks; mitigation: asynchronous workflow and clear SLAs.
Telemetry feedback loop may introduce circular dependencies; mitigation: ensure telemetry is used only for validation/recalibration, not for control.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of mission context schema (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints).
Validation of performance derivation algorithms against historical mission data and known outcomes.
Specification of baseline record format and metadata requirements.
Specification of audit log schema and cryptographic signing mechanism.
Specification of traceability query interface and supported filter criteria.
SLA agreement with Safety Services for validation of derived safety-critical requirements.
Performance benchmarks for derivation engine (latency, throughput, accuracy).
Test plan for input validation, baseline recording, history management, and traceability queries.
